options.why: Found $20\%$ of $30$ but forgot to add it back to the original.
Subtracted instead of adding, computing a $20\%$ decrease.
Found the $20\%$ that was removed instead of the amount remaining.
Added instead of subtracting, computing a $20\%$ increase.
Multiplied by $1.75$, forgetting to add the $175\%$ to the original $100\%$.
Doubled the amount ($200\%$) instead of increasing by $175\%$.

questions.question_text: James earns $\$850$ per week. He receives a $4\%$ pay rise. What is his new weekly wage?

options.why: Found the $4\%$ raise itself but did not add it to the original wage.
Subtracted $4\%$, giving a pay cut instead of a rise.

questions.question_text: The height of a plant increases by $150\%$. The original height was $70$ cm. Ravi multiplies by $1.5$ and gets $105$ cm. What is the correct new height?

options.text: $175$ cm; increasing by $150\%$ means multiplying by $2.5$.

options.why: Treated $150\%$ as the whole multiplier, forgetting to add the original $100\%$.
